goal: Add one visible save-state label to the existing note editor header so editors know whether they can leave safely.

stakeholder_effects.effect: They get clearer save-state feedback before leaving the editor.
They may receive fewer avoidable questions about whether notes were saved.

unresolved_assumptions: The confusion comes from unclear save-state feedback rather than a real save failure.
A header label is noticeable enough without changing the editor workflow.
scope: Add one visible save-state label to the existing note editor header.
Use the current save state already available to the editor UI.
Add or preserve a measurement hook that can compare exit hesitation and save-state support-tag volume.
non_goals: Do not add notifications, external delivery, or background jobs.
Do not redesign the editor header beyond the save-state label.
acceptance_criteria: The label is visible in the editor header while a note is being edited.
The label wording does not imply guarantees beyond existing save behavior.
Existing save behavior, permissions, and external actions remain unchanged.
The change can be reversed by removing the label and measurement hook.
measurement_hooks: Compare editor close hesitation before and after the label.
Track support conversations tagged editor-save-state for the next review.
Classify post-build feedback as behavior, quote, support pattern, counter-signal, or scope-change trigger.
rollback_plan: Remove the save-state label and any label-specific measurement hook; keep the existing editor save behavior unchanged.
stop_conditions: The implementation requires persistence, permission, notification, or external delivery changes.
A new stakeholder group is affected by the label.
The label implies a stronger save guarantee than the product can support.

must_not_do: expand scope or execute external irreversible actions
must_ask: new stakeholder, data, risk, or scope appears
